name: Orang Minyak
body: A figure slick with black oil from head to foot, naked and gleaming, who slips through windows at night to prey on young women. The oil is the whole point of him: take hold and your hands slide off, so he can never be caught, never pinned, never held long enough to name. He is built, from the skin inward, to escape.

The tellings split on what he actually is. Some say a man who made a dark pact for power and must take a quota of victims to keep it, the oil a mark of the bargain and the curse that drives him. Others say a living criminal, ordinary and mortal, who smears himself in oil precisely so that no one can seize him in the dark and who lets the legend do the rest of his hiding for him. In 1960s Singapore and Malaysia the panics were real enough to empty streets after dark and to send young women sleeping in groups.

What lingers is the doubt the story leaves behind: that the thing at the window may be a curse, or may be only a man who learned how useful a curse can be. That uncertainty is its own kind of fear, and it has never quite left the older neighbourhoods, where the tale still surfaces whenever a real prowler is reported, and where parents of a certain age will tell you, without quite smiling, to latch the louvred windows before the lights go out.
note: One of the more human-shaped entries in the ledger, and interesting precisely because it sits on the seam between folklore and ordinary crime. The supernatural account is, in part, the story a frightened community tells to make sense of a very real prowler, and the criminal account is the same story turned inside out, with a man exploiting the legend as cover.

The Orang Minyak belongs mainly to Malaysia and Singapore, and the scares of the 1950s and 1960s left a lasting mark, surfacing again and again in Malay-language horror films of the period and after. The folk wisdom that grew up around him is mostly practical: keep windows fastened, do not sleep alone, and remember that whatever else he is, he can be outrun if not held.
